stem: A energy power grid uses 2 parallel generators for redundancy. The power maintained requires at least one generator to function. generator 1 has a reliability between 70% and 78% (uncertain due to limited operational data). generator 2 has a reliability of 95%. What is the overall probability that the power maintained?

ground_truth.answer: P(power maintained) ranges from 0.985 to 0.989. Point estimate: 0.987.

solution_steps: P(system works) = 1 - P(all 2 generators fail simultaneously).
At lower bound: P(all fail) = 0.3 × 0.05 = 0.015. P(works) = 0.985.
At upper bound: P(all fail) = 0.22 × 0.05 = 0.011. P(works) = 0.989.
common_failure_modes: Multiplying individual reliabilities instead of computing 1 - P(all fail)
Using a single reliability for the uncertain component without propagating uncertainty
Treating the system as a series (all must work) rather than parallel (any one suffices)
